stamp_rationale: Deliverables shipped in main via commit 10bd394 (2026-04-22). Four prior council rounds produced only council-bug objections (see COUNCIL_ESCALATION.md and commits c87b4df, c778471, 8276e22). After fix-council-enforcement landed (967c98e + 5522e24), retroactive approval is stamped based on actual evidence.

verdicts.reason: Three scripts at repo root (ux_completeness.py, mobile_usability.py, cult_status.py) implement the lens checks per plan.md. Portfolio hit-rates table in changes.md documents real regressions caught across 5 projects (naval-scribe table-overflow, focus-ring on all 5, markdown-deck correctly passing cult_status). Prior BUGS objections were a false-positive (already-handled wrapper case) and a goalpost move (false-positive pivoted to false-negative) — both documented in COUNCIL_ESCALATION.md resolutions.
verdicts.evidence: ux_completeness.py:128, mobile_usability.py:124, cult_status.py:146; experiments/infra-yolo-evals/changes.md portfolio-hit-rates table

verdicts.reason: All three scripts validate that os.path.realpath(sys.argv[1]) is contained within REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)) before any open() call. Tested end-to-end: /etc/passwd rejected, ../../etc/passwd canonicalized and rejected, legit naval-scribe/index.html works. Pattern codified as a standing rule in learnings.md:30 ('Internal verifier path containment').

CLI output is clear: [WARN] <check>: <description> per issue, then PASS or WARNINGS: N summary. --help flag prints a full usage/check description. Exit codes documented (0 always advisory, 1 on file errors).
verdicts.evidence: mobile_usability.py:18-36 HELP text; mobile_usability.py:115-120 output logic

verdicts.reason: experiments/infra-yolo-evals/README.md documents CLI usage, exact parser rules (SECTION_RE, ROW_RE regex patterns), and check descriptions. Each script has a docstring summarizing its checks. --help surfaces the same info at runtime.
verdicts.evidence: experiments/infra-yolo-evals/README.md (55 lines); script docstrings lines 1-16 in each file

verdicts.reason: Lenses catch recurring YOLO-project oversights that existing tests (test_project.py, eval_bugs.py, security_scan.py) miss. Portfolio hit rates across 5 projects confirm real signal: focus-ring fires on all 5 (known antipattern), table-overflow caught naval-scribe regression, markdown-deck correctly graded PASS by cult_status. Advisory mode means the lenses never block builds but surface actionable feedback.

Internal build infrastructure is explicitly exempt from the signature-move bar per feedback_utility_focus and the standing override pattern applied to infra-guardrails and fix-council-enforcement. cult_status.py itself encodes a differentiation rubric, which is meta-cool even though the tool itself is internal plumbing.
verdicts.evidence: feedback_utility_focus memory; prior COOL overrides at infra-guardrails (fc51b22) and fix-council-enforcement (967c98e)

verdicts.reason: Implementation followed learnings.md guidance: internal verifier path containment (repo-root check with realpath), zero-dep Python regex per the '100-200 line lens' scope constraint from plan.md, advisory-mode output to avoid blocking the build loop. Prior LESSONS vetoes (2 false positives) were overridden with evidence and are now auto-downgraded by council.py:enforce_lessons_precondition (shipped 967c98e).
verdicts.evidence: learnings.md:30 (Internal verifier path containment rule); ux_completeness.py:19, mobile_usability.py:19, cult_status.py:21 implementing the pattern
